race.name: Panorama Nordic Loppet
race.tagline: A small-field gem in the Columbia Valley — ski beneath the Purcell Mountains with a hot springs finish reward.

vitals.registration: Opens in autumn; small field fills quickly
vitals.aid_stations: Two stations with water and snacks

climate.description: Early February in the Columbia Valley brings cold, dry continental conditions. Temperatures typically -15°C to -5°C. The valley location provides some shelter from major storms but guarantees reliable cold.
challenges: Cold starts in the valley bottom before warming with elevation gain
Dry powder snow requires specific wax selection
Remote location requires planning for travel

course.primary: A 25km loop through the trail network near Panorama Mountain Resort in the Columbia Valley. The course winds through subalpine forest with views of the Purcell and Rocky Mountain ranges. Moderate elevation gain with rolling terrain suited to intermediate skiers.
features: Views of both the Purcell and Rocky Mountain ranges
Quiet forest trails through pristine Columbia Valley wilderness
Access to Panorama hot springs and resort village post-race
Small, intimate field with a friendly community atmosphere
Good introductory distance for newer marathon skiers

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A tiny but scenic loppet in one of BC's most beautiful mountain valleys. The setting is spectacular but the event lacks the field size, organization, and competitive depth of established races. A destination for scenery seekers, not podium chasers.

history.summary: A young event started around 2012 by the local Nordic club to promote cross-country skiing in the Columbia Valley. Panorama is better known as an alpine ski resort, but the area has a small but dedicated Nordic community. The race remains a grassroots affair, organized by volunteers and drawing primarily from the Invermere and Windermere communities.
notable_facts: Panorama Mountain Resort is primarily known for alpine skiing
The Columbia Valley has a small but passionate Nordic ski community
Invermere hot springs are a popular post-race destination
One of the few organized Nordic races in the East Kootenay region
